children.name: span
children.textContent: The premise of this book is that it is easier to recognize other people's mistakes than our own. The observation led to a program of research, along with related developments in

children.name: span
children.textContent: , which has provided the tools for understanding decision-making under uncertainty.

children.name: span
children.textContent: Two distinct systems govern our thinking.

children.name: span
children.textContent: operates automatically with little effort, while

children.name: span
children.textContent: allocates attention to difficult tasks — and often simply endorses what System 1 suggests.

children.name: span
children.textContent: This research was developed with my long-time collaborator

children.name: span
children.textContent: , whose work on judgment under uncertainty transformed economics, medicine, and public policy.

children.name: span
children.textContent: A key insight that emerged from this collaboration was

children.name: span
children.textContent: , which replaced classical expected utility theory with a more accurate model of human choice.

children.name: span
children.textContent: The premise of this book is that it is easier to recognize other people's mistakes than our own. The observation led to a program of research, along with related developments in

children.name: span
children.textContent: , which has provided the tools for understanding decision-making under uncertainty.

children.name: span
children.textContent: Two distinct systems govern our thinking.

children.name: span
children.textContent: operates automatically with little effort, while

children.name: span
children.textContent: allocates attention to difficult tasks — and often simply endorses what System 1 suggests.

children.name: span
children.textContent: This research was developed with my long-time collaborator

children.name: span
children.textContent: , whose work on judgment under uncertainty transformed economics, medicine, and public policy.

children.name: span
children.textContent: A key insight that emerged from this collaboration was

children.name: span
children.textContent: , which replaced classical expected utility theory with a more accurate model of human choice.

children.name: p
children.textContent: "Availability heuristic" in Ch.12 directly parallels Sapiens Ch.6 on how humans distort historical memory.
